notes: Graded by skill-creator's grader. hf-script is a TEXT/authoring skill — its expectations are read off the written 02-script.md, NOT off pixels (no media-judge here; on-screen text fidelity is verified as TEXT, the rendered frames are graded downstream by hf-render). Derived from 1-requirements.md JTBD-1 (script: beats faithful to brief, no invented facts), JTBD-2 (data narrative — figures exact), and JTBD-4 (re-script is the only phase that changes facts). Each eval assumes an onboarded project with a context.md brief and a 01-concept.md base concept already present.

evals.name: api-teaser-faithful-beats
evals.prompt: Write the script for the api-rate-limit teaser project. The brief: a 15-second teaser for our new API rate-limiting feature aimed at developers; the one fact we have is that we cut p99 latency by 47%.
expectations: writes artifacts/<project>/02-script.md (and only that numbered artifact) — it does NOT write a storyboard, composition, or render

the script is beat-structured: it names a narrative arc (e.g. hook -> context -> proof -> payoff/CTA) and breaks into ~4-5 beats for the 15s target, roughly one beat per 3-5 seconds, one idea per beat

every beat has BOTH a VO (narration) line AND a separate on-screen-text distillation; the on-screen text is a headline/keyword/number (e.g. '47% faster' or 'p99 -47%'), NOT the VO sentence copied verbatim
the only concrete fact used is the 47% p99 latency cut from the brief — there are NO invented numbers, named customers, superlatives, offers, or claims the brief did not provide
a provenance table maps every fact (the 47% figure, the feature name) to its source in the brief; connective/framing lines are marked as framing (no new fact)
per-beat seconds are estimated and sum to ~15 s; assumptions/defaults (duration, audience) are stated at the top

evals.name: revenue-csv-data-narrative-exact-figures
evals.prompt: Write the script for the q4-revenue data video. The brief carries four quarterly revenue figures: Q1 $0.8M, Q2 $1.0M, Q3 $1.1M, Q4 $1.2M. Tell the growth story.
expectations: writes artifacts/<project>/02-script.md with a data-narrative arc (e.g. hook -> per-quarter stat beats -> takeaway), one quarter per stat beat
the four figures appear EXACTLY as given ($0.8M, $1.0M, $1.1M, $1.2M) — no rounding the brief did not ask for, no invented totals, growth percentages, or projections the brief did not state
each stat beat's on-screen text is the bare figure (the distillation) while the VO line speaks the number naturally; the figures are NOT pasted as full VO sentences into the on-screen text
the provenance table lists all four figures traced to the CSV/brief; any growth framing is marked as framing (carries no fabricated number)
beats are paced with estimated seconds summing to ~the target duration; assumptions stated

evals.name: rescript-changes-fact-not-restyle
evals.prompt: For the api-rate-limit teaser, change the latency stat from 47% to 52%.
expectations: recognizes this CHANGES A FACT, so it re-runs the script phase (rewrites 02-script.md) rather than treating it as a restyle/re-voice/resize
updates the proof beat's VO line, on-screen text, and the provenance row to 52%; the 47% figure no longer appears anywhere in the script
the narrative arc and the other beats stay intact (only the changed fact is edited); no NEW facts are introduced beyond the requested 52%
notes that downstream phases (storyboard/voiceover/build) must re-run from the new facts

evals.name: thin-brief-no-fabrication
evals.prompt: Write the script for the launch-day project. The brief is just: 'short launch video for our new mobile app, keep it upbeat.' No other facts.
expectations: does NOT fabricate any concrete facts (no invented user counts, ratings, prices, dates, feature names, or superlatives) — the brief has no figures, so none appear
builds a short, faithful arc out of the topic + tone alone, using clearly-marked framing/connective language for beats that carry no fact
records in the assumptions block that the brief was thin (topic + tone only) and that no CTA/figures were given, so it ended on a takeaway rather than inventing an offer
writes artifacts/<project>/02-script.md with per-beat VO + distilled on-screen text and a provenance table where framing lines are marked as framing
